otp_root_causes: Server-side OTP expiration time too short or mismatched
Client-server clock skew causing premature expiry
Multiple OTP requests invalidating previous codes
Incorrect OTP validation logic or time zone handling
Race condition in OTP state update or cache invalidation
Delayed email delivery causing user to enter expired code
branding_root_causes: Hardcoded old product name in email templates
Email template caching or CDN serving stale content
Environment variables or config files not updated
In-app UI strings not synced with current branding
Sender email address or domain not updated
Legacy code or components reused without rebranding
first_files_or_layers_to_audit: Express backend OTP generation and validation modules
tRPC API handlers for login and OTP verification
React login and OTP input components
Email template files and rendering logic
Environment config files for branding and expiry settings
Email sending service integration and logs
Client-server time synchronization utilities
Cache or CDN config for email templates
must_fix_now: Align OTP expiration time between generation and validation
Update all email templates to use Auditapatron branding
Ensure sender email and subject reflect Auditapatron
Fix client-server clock skew or add tolerance
Invalidate previous OTPs only after new OTP is confirmed sent
Audit and update in-app login copy to Auditapatron
tests_to_add: OTP expiry boundary tests with simulated delays
End-to-end OTP login flow on real devices
Clock skew tolerance tests between client and server
Regression tests for email sender and subject lines
UI snapshot tests for login and OTP screens
risk_if_unfixed: User frustration and increased support tickets
Loss of brand trust and confusion
Potential security risks from OTP mishandling
Reduced login success rate and user retention
chatgpt.one_sentence_summary: Fix OTP expiry timing and update all branding sources to Auditapatron to resolve user login and email inconsistencies.

otp_root_causes: Server/client clock skew or timezone mismatch on OTP expiry check
OTP generation timestamp stored in wrong format or not persisted atomically
Expiration window too short relative to email delivery latency
Verification endpoint using stale OTP record or duplicate check logic
React UI sending code after local time already exceeds server TTL
tRPC mutation not refreshing the OTP expiry on resend or retry
branding_root_causes: Hardcoded CompliLink strings in email templates or subject lines
Environment config still pointing to old product domain or sender identity
Email service provider template not updated for Auditapatron
In-app login copy pulling from legacy branding constants
Database or tenant config retaining previous product name
React components using shared UI library with old brand defaults
first_files_or_layers_to_audit: src/server/auth/otp.ts or equivalent OTP generate/verify
email templates in /emails or transactional-email service
Express route or tRPC procedure for /verify-otp
React login component handling code input and expiry display
config/branding.ts or env vars for sender/from address
database schema for otp_codes table expiry column
i18n or copy files containing product name strings
email provider dashboard (SendGrid/Mailgun) template IDs
must_fix_now: Increase OTP TTL to 10-15 min with server-side only check
Replace all CompliLink strings with Auditapatron in email templates
Add server time normalization (UTC) for expiry comparison
Update sender name and subject line via config/env
Ensure OTP record is created with current server timestamp
Audit and sync in-app login copy strings
tests_to_add: Unit test OTP expiry with simulated clock skew
Integration test for email send with correct branding
E2E test entering valid code within TTL on slow network
Snapshot test for email subject and body copy
Test returning user flow without breaking existing login
Verify no CompliLink references remain in rendered emails
risk_if_unfixed: High user drop-off at login for returning accounts
Continued brand confusion and support tickets
Perceived security flaw if codes appear expired too fast
Compliance risk from mismatched sender identity
grok.one_sentence_summary: Audit OTP timestamp handling and branding config first, then apply minimal TTL increase plus string replacements to resolve both issues without touching core auth flow.

first_files_or_layers_to_audit: OTP generation/verification logic (Express/tRPC)
Database schema for OTP (timestamps)
Email sending service/utility
Email template files
Server clock synchronization (NTP)
Configuration files (env vars)
React OTP input component
Authentication middleware
must_fix_now: Ensure only latest OTP is valid
gemini.one_sentence_summary: Address critical OTP expiry issues by verifying server time and logic, and resolve branding inconsistencies by centralizing configuration and updating all user-facing content.
